title: phacelia sericea subsp. ciliosa
intro: Phacelia sericea subsp. ciliosa, also known as silky phacelia, is a perennial plant in the borage family native to the southwestern United States. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been shown to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Phacelia sericea subsp. ciliosa can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used topically as a salve or oil to promote skin health and well-being.
